title: Multiply with 9
essential_question: What strategies can you use to multiply with 9?
strategies: Digits of 9s products add up to 9
Tens digit = n − 1; ones digit = 10 − n

learn.title: Strategy: Subtract from ×10
learn.content: 9 times a number = 10 times that number minus the number. 9 × 6 = 10 × 6 − 6 = 60 − 6 = 54.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.9 'Multiply with 9' pp.171-174
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic E — Multiplying with the Unit of 9
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Digit Sum Pattern
learn.content: The digits of every ×9 product add up to 9: 9→9, 18→1+8=9, 27→2+7=9, 36→3+6=9, 45→4+5=9. Use it to check your answer.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.9 'Multiply with 9' pp.171-174
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic E — Multiplying with the Unit of 9
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Two-Digit Rule for 9 × n
learn.content: For 9 × n (where n is 1–10): tens digit = n − 1, ones digit = 10 − n. Example: 9 × 7 → tens = 6, ones = 3 → 63. Always check with the digit sum (6 + 3 = 9).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.9 'Multiply with 9' pp.171-174
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic E — Multiplying with the Unit of 9
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Finger Trick for 9s
learn.content: Hold up 10 fingers. To find 9 × n, fold down the n-th finger. Fingers LEFT of it = tens digit; fingers RIGHT of it = ones digit. Example: 9 × 4 — fold 4th finger: 3 fingers left, 6 fingers right → 36.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.9 'Multiply with 9' pp.171-174
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic E — Multiplying with the Unit of 9
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: ×9 두 자리 규칙 (가장 강력한 패턴)
learn.content: 9 × n의 두 자리는 항상 두 가지 규칙으로 결정됨: 1) 십의 자리 = (n − 1). 2) 일의 자리 = (10 − n). 두 자리 합은 항상 9. 예: 9 × 4 → 십=3, 일=6 → 36 (3+6=9 ✓). 예: 9 × 7 → 십=6, 일=3 → 63 (6+3=9 ✓). 예: 9 × 8 → 십=7, 일=2 → 72 (7+2=9 ✓). 흔한 실수: 십의 자리에 (n−1) 대신 n을 그대로 쓰는 경우.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.9 'Multiply with 9' pp.171-174
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic E — Multiplying with the Unit of 9
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.9 'Multiply with 9' pp.171-174
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic E — Multiplying with the Unit of 9
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: ×9 즉답 전략 4가지 + 검증법
learn.content: 전략 1 두 자리 규칙: 십=n−1, 일=10−n. 전략 2 ×10 빼기: 9n = 10n − n. 전략 3 손가락 트릭: 손가락 10개 펴고 n번째 손가락 접기. 왼쪽=십, 오른쪽=일. 전략 4 분배 5+4: 9 × n = (5n) + (4n). 예 9×8 = 40+32 = 72. 🔍 검증법 (자릿수 합 검사): 정답이라면 두 자리 합 = 9.   예: 9 × 4 = 36 → 3+6=9 ✓. 잘못된 답 9 × 5 = 48 → 4+8=12 ≠ 9 ❌.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.9 'Multiply with 9' pp.171-174

try.question: Using the two-digit rule: 9 × 8 → tens digit = 8 − 1 = ?, ones digit = 10 − 8 = ?

try.question: A student writes 9 × 5 = 48. Use the digit sum check: 4 + 8 = 12. Is the answer right?
try.explanation: 4 + 8 = 12, not 9. A correct ×9 product has digits summing to 9. Actual answer: 9 × 5 = 45 (4 + 5 = 9 ✓).
hints: Digits of a ×9 product must sum to 9.

feedback.incorrect: Digits must sum to 9. Correct answer is 45.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.9 'Multiply with 9' pp.171-174
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic E — Multiplying with the Unit of 9
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: A baseball team has 9 players. How many players on 3 teams?
hints: Players per team × number of teams.

hints: Put the two digits together: 5 and 4.

practice_r2.question: True or False: 9 × 8 is even.
practice_r2.explanation: True — 9 × 8 = 72. 8 is even, so the product is even.

feedback.incorrect: True. 8 is even, so 9 × 8 = 72 is even.

practice_r2.question: Find the difference: 813 − 467.
practice_r2.explanation: 813−467: 3−7 borrow → 13−7=6; tens 0−6 borrow → 10−6=4 (after lending); hundreds 7−4=3. Result: 346.
hints: Borrow across the zero in tens.
